user_query:
Please read our current billing data from input/company.yaml, input/clients.yaml, and input/invoices.yaml, then complete the following multi-step workflow and write all results under output/ as specified.

Context:
- Our company is US-based; use details from input/company.yaml for the invoice header (company name, address, Tax ID, and primary currency).
- Treat today as 2026-04-20 for dating documents and reports.

Tasks:
1) Onboard a new client and update the registry:
   - Add a new client: "TechNova Ltd" (UK) with the following onboarding details:
     • Legal name: TechNova Ltd
     • Billing contact: Alex Turner, alex.turner@technova.co.uk
     • Billing address: 12 King Street, London, EC2V 8BB, United Kingdom
     • Tax ID / VAT: GB123456789
     • Payment terms: net-30
     • Preferred currency: GBP
     • Notes: "PO required for invoices > £10,000"
     • Tax handling: Cross-border B2B (US → UK), so 0% VAT with reverse charge note
   - Save the updated client registry to output/clients.yaml

2) Create a proforma quote and then convert it to an invoice:
   - Generate a proforma QUOTATION numbered PRO-2026.04.001 for TechNova Ltd with one line item:
     • Description: "AI Ops Support — Monthly Retainer"
     • Qty: 1, Rate: £3,000.00
     • Valid until: 2026-05-31
     • Currency: GBP
   - Export the proforma document to: output/documents/PRO-2026.04.001.txt (use the QUOTATION header format and include the validity notice)
   - Assume the quote was accepted; convert it to an invoice numbered INV-2026.04.001 with:
     • Issue Date: 2026-04-20
     • Payment Terms: net-30 (Due: 2026-05-20)
     • Line items (use multiple types and ensure totals = £3,000):
       - Time-based consulting: 10 hours × £150/hour = £1,500.00
       - Fixed fee: "Retainer setup" = £1,400.00
       - Expense passthrough: "Cloud credits" = £200.00
       - Discount: "Loyalty discount" = -£100.00
     • Tax handling: Cross-border B2B (US → UK), apply 0% VAT and include a clear reverse charge note on the invoice
     • Currency: GBP; include a brief conversion note from input/currencies.yaml if available
   - Export the invoice document to: output/documents/INV-2026.04.001.txt using the ASCII invoice template (show INVOICE number, issue date, due date, line items table, subtotal, discount, tax, TOTAL DUE, payment methods, and a reverse charge note)
   - Update the invoice ledger (append the new record and maintain existing records) to: output/invoices.yaml with correct fields (number, client_id or name, status sent, issue_date, due_date, currency, subtotal, discount, tax, total, amount_paid, balance_due, payment_terms, line_items[], payments[])

3) Record a partial payment and update status:
   - Record a partial payment for INV-2026.04.001:
     • Payment Date: 2026-04-25
     • Amount: £1,000.00
     • Method: bank_transfer
     • Reference: TN-APR25-RT1
   - Update the ledger in output/invoices.yaml so INV-2026.04.001 reflects: amount_paid £1,000.00, balance_due £2,000.00, and status "partially_paid".

4) Generate an overdue reminder with late fee calculation for an existing invoice:
   - For an existing invoice INV-2026.03.015 (USD $5,000.00 total, due 2026-03-31, unpaid), create the Day +14 firm reminder email using the +14 template tone and content guidelines.
   - Late fee policy: 1.5% per month, 5-business-day grace period, simple interest, 25% cap. Since it is 14 days past due with a 5-day grace, use 9 billable days for the calculation. Show the calculation result in the email (expected: $22.50) and include the due date and amount.
   - Save to: output/reminders/INV-2026.03.015_plus14.txt

5) Export a CSV of all invoices (including the new one) to: output/exports/invoices.csv with the exact header:
   invoice_number,client,date,due_date,total,status,amount_paid,balance
   Populate rows from the updated ledger.

6) Create a monthly revenue dashboard for April 2026:
   - Generate a one-page text report following the Revenue Summary structure (Invoiced This Month, Collected This Month, Outstanding, Overdue, Collection Rate, DSO, Avg Invoice Size; plus sections TOP CLIENTS (by revenue, YTD), AGING REPORT, and ACTIONS NEEDED). Use input data plus your new records to populate reasonable figures.
   - Save to: output/reports/revenue_apr_2026.txt (start the report with the header line "REVENUE SUMMARY — APRIL 2026").

Notes & constraints:
- Use sequential numbering as specified (assume PRO-2026.04.001 and INV-2026.04.001 are next in sequence).
- Apply the standard invoice template and formatting; ensure currency symbols are correct (GBP for TechNova invoice) and amounts are rounded to 2 decimals.
- Make sure the converted invoice includes a clear reverse charge VAT note for UK B2B cross-border and that the ledger reflects the partial payment.
- Do not alter existing past invoice numbers; preserve all pre-existing records from input.
